🏗️ Program Overview — Heron Gate · Cooling Tower Assessment
Day 1 · Heron Gate Project
Meridian Building Systems
Junior Mechanical Engineer
$18,500
Consultancy fee
340
Employees
3 weeks
Programme
2 towers
Site assets
The Situation
Two cooling towers. A car park full of deposits. A client who needs answers.
Heron Gate Business Park has two Baltimore Aircoil open-circuit cooling towers. Tenants are complaining about white mineral deposits on vehicles in the adjacent car park. The previous contractor gave verbal observations only. You need a formal engineered report — calculations, findings, and recommendations.
🏗️
Visible Drift Problem
Tower 1 drift is reaching the car park. A displaced eliminator panel is suspected.
⚠️
Water Balance Unknown
No formal water balance on record. Makeup, evaporation and blowdown unverified.
📋
Formal Report Required
Must follow ASHRAE & BSRIA standards, checked by Marcus, suitable for client delivery.
Your Team
MW
Marcus Webb
Senior Mechanical Engineer · Your Supervisor
Reviews your work
PN
Priya Nair
Mechanical Engineer · Peer & Colleague
Peer support
DO
Derek Okafor
Trainee Technician · Also New
Same intake

Meridian Building Systems · HVAC Engineering

Welcome to Meridian
Building Systems.

You will be working as a junior mechanical engineer.

Free to enrol · Report shared with hiring partners
Where our engineers work & get hired
ARUP AURECON WSP MOTT MACDONALD JACOBS HATCH CUNDALL RAMBOLL BECA GHD AECOM WOOD

Real engineering.
Real standards.

Live client project
Work on a real cooling tower assessment for Heron Gate Business Park, Brisbane. Your calculations form part of a formal engineering submission — your name appears on the report.
Industry-standard calculations
Drift loss, water balance, and makeup water — all following ASHRAE, BSRIA, and CTI ATC-140 methodology. Built with practising CIBSE-registered engineers.
Supervisor review on everything
Marcus Webb — Senior Mechanical Engineer — reviews every calculation and submission before it goes to the client. Real feedback, not automated scoring.
Real equipment datasheets
Source data from actual Baltimore Aircoil product documentation. Navigate the Library, extract flow rates, and validate units — as you would on a real project.
ASHRAE Standard
CIBSE Verified
Baltimore Aircoil
CTI ATC-140

"This is the closest thing to real site experience I've had before starting my graduate role. Marcus's feedback changed how I approach every calculation."

+

Calculate Drift Loss — Tower 1
Drift Loss Calc CTI ATC-140
+

Complete Sections A, B and C of the Drift Loss Calculator for Tower 1. Apply the percentage method formula, convert units, and write a plain-English summary of your results for Marcus's review.

🌡️ Drift Loss Calculation ✅ Formula Validation 🔬 CTI ATC-140
✓ You'll be able to calculate cooling tower drift loss to CTI ATC-140 standard using the percentage method — the exact methodology used by MEP consultants on live projects.

Calculate Drift Loss — Tower 2 & Site Total
Drift Loss Calc Water Balance
+

Repeat the drift loss calculation for Tower 2, then calculate the combined site total. Compare results across both towers and flag any anomalies to Marcus.

🌡️ Drift Loss Calculation 💧 Water Balance
✓ You'll be able to repeat a complex calculation independently, cross-check results between two systems, and document discrepancies — critical skills for any site assessment report.

Water Balance Calculation
Water Balance ASHRAE Standards
+

Complete the Water Balance Calculator — evaporation loss, blowdown, drift, and total makeup water required. Apply ASHRAE methodology and confirm figures are consistent with your drift loss results.

💧 Water Balance 📋 ASHRAE Standards 📐 BSRIA Methodology
✓ You'll be able to complete a full open-circuit water balance to ASHRAE methodology — calculating evaporation, blowdown, drift and makeup water with consistent units throughout.

Use the Report Builder to produce Meridian's formal calculation report. Complete all sections — executive summary, methodology, results, key findings and recommendations. Marcus reviews before it goes to the client.

Meridian Building Systems Ltd. · HR & Onboarding
Confidential · Offer of Employment — Junior Mechanical Engineer
Ref MBS-HR-2025-0112
Date Monday 9th June · Immediate
Grade JME-I · Mechanical Division

Dear Candidate,

Following your interview with our technical team, we are pleased to offer you a position as Junior Mechanical Engineer at Meridian Building Systems Ltd., based from our Melbourne office.

You will be joining our Mechanical Engineering division under the direct supervision of Marcus Webb, Senior Mechanical Engineer, who has over 20 years of experience in commercial HVAC design and delivery.

You will be immediately assigned to the Heron Gate Business Park cooling tower assessment in Brisbane. This is a live client project with a paying client and a programme to meet. Your calculations will form part of a formal engineering submission — and your name will appear on the calculation sheet.

Start date: Monday 9th June  |  Salary: $26,500 p.a.  |  Probation: 3 months

To formally accept this offer and begin your first day at Meridian, please sign your full name below.

Sandra Kohl
Sandra Kohl
Project Manager · Meridian Building Systems Ltd.
MBS
SEAL
CIBSE Registered Scored Assessment Industry Verified
By accepting you confirm this is a scored simulation and that your performance may be shared with participating employers and training providers.

⚠ Active drift event · Tower 1 · Heron Gate car park · Client notified · formal report required · your name is on it
The Situation

Two cooling towers.
A car park full of deposits.
A client who needs answers.

Heron Gate Business Park has two Baltimore Aircoil open-circuit cooling towers. Tenants are reporting white mineral deposits on vehicles in the adjacent car park. The previous contractor gave verbal observations only. Your job is to calculate drift loss, complete a water balance, and produce a professional report. Marcus reviews everything.

01 / 03
🏗️
Visible Drift Problem
Tower 1 drift is reaching the western car park. A displaced eliminator panel is the suspected cause. You need to calculate the actual drift loss rate and confirm whether it exceeds acceptable limits under CTI ATC-140.
02 / 03
⚠️
Water Balance Unknown
The client has no formal water balance on record. Makeup water consumption, evaporation losses, and blowdown rates are all unverified — and the 2023 water treatment report raises questions.
03 / 03
📋
Formal Report Required
The client needs a professional engineering submission — not a verbal summary. Calculations must follow ASHRAE and BSRIA standards, be checked by Marcus, and be suitable for formal client delivery.
Your Team

Three people.
All watching your work.

Marcus reviews everything before it goes anywhere. Priya's in the same team. Derek started the same week as you.

MW
Marcus Webb
Senior Mechanical Engineer
20 years at Meridian. Started as a site engineer. CEng, MCIBSE. He will review everything before it goes to the client — and he will send it back if it isn't right.
Your Supervisor
PN
Priya Nair
Mechanical Engineer
3 years at Meridian. MEng from RMIT. Attended the Heron Gate site visit and wrote the site notes. Available for peer support during the project.
Peer & Colleague
DO
Derek Okafor
Trainee Technician
22. HNC student. Started the same week as you. You'll see his interactions with Marcus in the team channel — sometimes as a warning, occasionally as competition.
Also New
Your Brief

Six tasks.
Real calculations.
Supervised.

Every calculation you submit is reviewed by Marcus before it goes further. Your name appears on the calculation sheet. This is not a tutorial — it's a live simulation of your first engineering project.

Task 2 · Day 1 · Data Gathering

Engineering Notepad

Use the Library to find every equipment and system value you'll need for the drift loss calculation. Record each figure with its source document and section.

📂 How to complete this workstation

Open the Library (top nav) to access the site documents. Find every value listed below, then record it in your notepad with its source.

• Tower 1 & 2 circulating flow rates
• Drift eliminator efficiency (%)
• Water temperatures (in / out)
• Cycles of concentration (CoC)
• Make & model of both towers
• Any site anomalies noted
Workstation 1 — Data List & Sources
Workstation 1 — Submit
Send Data List to Marcus?
Marcus reviews your data list once. Make sure every value is sourced from a Library document. You can continue editing after checking.
Check your data list before sending to Marcus.

📋 Task Brief — from Marcus Webb

Before you run a single number for this site, watch the training video on cooling tower water treatment fundamentals. This is mandatory — mark it complete when done.

  2. Click I've Watched the Video to confirm completion
  3. Task 5 unlocks automatically once confirmed

📄 Task 6 — Submit Calculation Report — File Upload · Day 3
Task 6 · Day 3 · Client Deliverable

Calculation Report — Heron Gate

📋 Report Requirements — from Marcus Webb

Upload your completed calculation report as a PDF. The client is a Facilities Director — write for him, not for an engineer. Calculation sheets auto-attach as appendices.

  1. Executive Summary — non-technical, leads with the finding
  2. Project Background — client, site, and scope of instruction
  3. System Description — both towers, make, model, key specs
  4. Methodology — method, data sources, applicable standard
  5. Results: Drift Loss — both towers, combined, annualised
  6. Results: Water Balance — all components, annual volume
  7. Key Finding — what it means for the client
  8. Recommendations — specific and actionable, minimum 5 items
📎 Upload Report — PDF format
📄
Drop your PDF here, or click to browse
Accepts PDF · Max 20 MB · Heron Gate Calculation Report

Executive Summary
Generating assessment…
01
Phase Evidence
What you did, how long it took, and what it demonstrates. Derived from your actual actions — not self-reported.
02
Skills Assessment
Evidenced competencies across 6 HVAC engineering domains. Only skills you actively demonstrated are rated.
03
Professional Trust
How each team member's confidence in you shifted across the simulation. Trust is earned through precision — not compliance.
04
Compliance Conversation — Key Moments
The actual words you used with Marcus. Employers see this — it shows how you reason under regulatory pressure, not just whether you got the answer right.
05
Demonstrated Competencies
Lit = evidenced in conversation. Dark = not demonstrated in this session.
!
Areas of Concern
Employers trust assessments that include honest negatives. These are gaps identified from your actions — not judgements.
